data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion A1 (Anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH) [agent]. Target: the "Eval foundation: deterministic-scorer registry + per-reviewer dataset schema (2.1 + 2.3)" plan (relates to this ticket's shipped eval suite). Tool-grounded against src/rebar/llm/. Two COACHING findings (NIH-reuse + term-collision); the registry abstraction itself PASSES YAGNI/Rule-of-Three.

AFFIRMATIONS (A1 criteria this plan satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):
- YAGNI / serves a current done-definition: the scorer registry serves AC#2 (an unregistered/typo'd deterministic scorer name must FAIL the offline gate). This is a REAL current gap, not a hypothetical — validate_scorer today only checks the name is non-empty (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:120-123: `if not scorer.get("name"): ... return errs`), so a misspelled `recall_on_seeded_defcts` passes the offline gate silently. The registry closes that hole. Not premature.
- Rule-of-Three (>=3 existing call-sites): the scorer NAMES the registry must cover already appear across >=4 SHIPPED specs — emits_valid_findings (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:19, plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml:15), no_fire_on_good_cases / recall_on_seeded_defects (finder:21-28), cites_real_paths (code-quality.eval.yaml:22), recall_on_silent_drop / no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope (isf-finder). So a name->function table is justified by existing call-sites, not invented for a future. PASS.
- No config-surface proliferation: the dataset-case schema fields (id/corpus/expect/criterion/kind/pair/mode/note) and the gate knobs (epochs/gate/coverage_threshold) already exist as de-facto YAML keys in the three shipped specs; the plan VALIDATES the existing surface rather than adding a parallel one. No new config toggle.
- No golden-hammer / resume-driven / premature-optimization: stdlib-only pure functions over (case, structured_output)->bool/score, offline + deterministic (AC#5) — matches the module's existing "the pure logic here is stdlib-only and offline-testable" posture (eval.py:19-21). No trendy tech, no speculative optimization.

FINDING 1 (A1 — NIH / rebuild-vs-reuse of an EXISTING name->builder registry for shape-validation):
- [ ] Make the `emits_valid_findings` / `emits_valid_review_result` / `emits_valid_verdict` scorers DELEGATE to the already-shipped contract registry (`rebar.llm.contracts.response_model_for(name)` over `_CONTRACTS`) instead of re-implementing structured-output shape-validation in the new scorer module — name the registered contract each scorer validates against (e.g. emits_valid_findings -> `plan_review_findings`; emits_valid_review_result -> `plan_review_verification` / `review_result`; emits_valid_verdict -> `completion_verdict`), so "the output validates against the X contract" reuses ONE source of truth rather than forking it.
  - criteria: [A1]
  - location: plan "What" bullet 1 (scorer registry naming emits_valid_findings/emits_valid_review_result/emits_valid_verdict); plan AC#1; src/rebar/llm/contracts.py:24-39, 88-89; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:143-150; docs/reuse-surface.md:324-344
  - evidence: A name->builder registry that maps a serializable NAME to the Pydantic model the output must validate against ALREADY EXISTS and is a documented reuse seam — `contracts.register_contract(name, builder)` + `contracts.response_model_for(output_schema)` (contracts.py:24-39); the exact contracts these scorers would check are already registered: `plan_review_findings` / `plan_review_verification` / `plan_review_coach` (passes.py:143-150) and `review_result` / `completion_verdict` (contracts.py:88-89). The finder spec already DESCRIBES emits_valid_findings as "The output validates against the plan_review_findings contract" (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:19-20). Rebuilding shape-validation in eval_scorers.py would duplicate `response_model_for` and risk drift from the schema-pin tests that keep each model in lock-step with its JSON Schema (contracts.py:9-10). This is in-codebase production reuse (not a docs/experiments POC), so FP7 (designated-experiment carve-out) does NOT apply.
  - suggested_fix: In the registry, implement emits_valid_* as a thin wrapper: `model = contracts.response_model_for("plan_review_findings"); model.model_validate(structured_output)` returning bool — one wrapper per registered contract name, no bespoke field-by-field validation.

FINDING 2 (A1 — term collision / cargo-cult reuse of an existing loaded name for a different concept):
- [ ] Rename the eval-dataset BATCH unit so it does not reuse `scan_spec`, OR state explicitly that it is unrelated to the existing `scan_spec` operation — the codebase already has a `scan_spec` (an MCP/CLI batch spec-scan op that makes LIVE, billable LLM calls over a store's epics), and overloading the same identifier for an OFFLINE eval-dataset "one spec + N epic fixtures" batch unit invites confusion at every call/grep site.
  - criteria: [A1]
  - location: plan "What" bullet 2 ("the `scan_spec` BATCH unit (one spec + N epic fixtures)"); src/rebar/mcp_server.py:618-627; src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:119; CLAUDE.md:176; rebar.toml:25
  - evidence: `scan_spec(spec_text, batch_size)` is an EXISTING, distinct operation — "live LLM call(s) over the store's [epics]" (tests/interfaces/facades/test_mcp_output_schema_coverage.py:104), CLI `rebar scan-spec` (docs/llm-framework.md:346), gated MCP `scan_spec` (mcp_server.py:618). The plan's `scan_spec` names an OFFLINE deterministic eval fixture unit — opposite of "live, billable" (rebar.toml:25). Same token, two unrelated meanings = a cargo-cult/term-overloading smell. This is a naming clarity coaching item, not a load-bearing defect; if the eval batch unit is INTENDED to mirror the live op's fixture shape, say so.
  - suggested_fix: (empty — pick a non-colliding name, e.g. `scan_batch_case` or `spec_scan_fixture`, or add a one-line note disambiguating it from the live `scan_spec` op.)

No further A1 findings: the registry, dataset schema, and validation extensions are appropriately scoped to current done-definitions and reuse the shipped eval seam rather than reinventing it.
